primary_sources.title: Best Fit Bin Packing with Random Order Revisited
primary_sources.supported_fact: The random-order behavior of Best Fit depends materially on items larger than one third of capacity; the paper also identifies a monotonicity property in that restricted regime.
primary_sources.code_status: No author-maintained implementation was identified in this bounded primary-source audit; no code is imported.

primary_sources.title: A new and improved algorithm for online bin packing
primary_sources.supported_fact: Advanced Harmonic partitions items by size and uses multiple bin types for a class, including exact-size treatment for sufficiently large items.
primary_sources.code_status: No author-maintained implementation was identified in this bounded primary-source audit; no code is imported.

primary_sources.title: Online Bin Packing with Predictions
primary_sources.supported_fact: Frequency predictions can be traded against adversarial prediction error through an explicit consistency-versus-robustness design.
primary_sources.code_status: No author-maintained implementation was identified in this bounded primary-source audit; no code is imported.

primary_sources.title: Evolution of Heuristics official repository
primary_sources.supported_fact: Upstream EOH supports function, multi-function, and class templates; the vendored local BP adapter currently uses only a function template.
primary_sources.code_status: Audited as an interface option only; no upstream code was copied in this tick.

mechanisms.hypothesis: Separating paired order sensitivity by the proportion of items above one third of capacity will make FME counterexamples more discriminating than one aggregate order gap.

changes: Add large_dominant, mixed, and small_dominant development strata with fresh seeds.
Record per-stratum paired order gap and a large-item order-sensitivity feature.
Let the controller prioritize a counterexample comparison when a candidate improves one order regime but regresses another.

mechanisms.minimum_future_validation: Freeze a new development-only stratum manifest and verify that the descriptor separates at least two pre-existing distinct candidate codes before any LLM cohort is considered.
mechanisms.exit_condition: Reject the module if the new descriptor cannot distinguish existing behavior profiles or if its counterexamples duplicate the same ranking signature.
mechanisms.expected_effect: More informative feedback and safer order-generalization diagnosis; no quality-improvement claim is implied.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A score family that conditions on normalized current-item class and residual-capacity class can preserve complementary residual space under adversarial order better than an unstructured scalar score.

integration.required_contract: score(item_ratio, feasible_remaining_ratio, context) or a class-policy equivalent with explicit capacity.
integration.current_blocker: The current score(item, bins) contract cannot reliably normalize by original capacity after all bins have been used.
integration.safe_fallback: Keep the current stateless score contract and store the mechanism only as an external_teacher strategy card.

mechanisms.minimum_future_validation: First freeze the normalized adapter contract and a non-LLM static interface review; only then decide whether a new cohort is warranted.
mechanisms.exit_condition: Reject if implementation needs hard-coded capacity constants, future-item access, or problem-specific state that cannot be represented in a portable adapter.
mechanisms.expected_effect: Potentially reduces destructive use of residual capacities; this is a hypothesis, not an imported Advanced Harmonic implementation.

mechanisms.hypothesis: A policy may use only prefix-observed size frequencies plus a provenance-tagged memory prior when confidence is high, and must fall back to a stateless score when drift is high.

integration.required_contract: OnlinePackingPolicy.score(...) plus observe(pre_item_state, post_item_state), with a serialized prediction_error and confidence field.
integration.memory_rule: Any prior frequency profile is a dev-only strategy asset with source and applicability metadata, never a held-out-derived value.
integration.controller_rule: Low confidence suppresses prediction-guided transfer and records a repair or counterexample action.

mechanisms.minimum_future_validation: Freeze a no-lookahead adapter contract and demonstrate that all predictor inputs are available before the current placement; do not use a future frequency oracle.
mechanisms.exit_condition: Reject if any feature requires future sequence information, if confidence cannot be logged and replayed, or if the module cannot fall back to the stateless baseline.
mechanisms.expected_effect: A high-upside RAG/feedback mechanism for nonstationary order regimes, with no inherited theoretical guarantee for this empirical adaptation.

decision.reason: It is the only mapped mechanism that improves the existing feedback loop without changing the candidate API, using held-out data, or requiring a new LLM budget.
decision.deferred_reason: Both require a new normalized or stateful problem-adapter contract and therefore must not be silently mixed into the already frozen FME v2 proposal.
